description.value:
You have been called to become a Treespeaker. Answering this Call and receiving the benefits of this dedication feat usually requires you to commune with an elder tree for one week to receive your powers and instructions, though exceptions are sometimes made for extreme situations, such as when you are very far from the nearest elder tree, or unable to reach one for another reason. Exceptions are also made if the reason of the call is urgent enough that a one week communion would prevent you from fulfilling your calling. Regardless, you must return to the elder tree to complete your communion within one year of your calling, or you lose all benefits of this archetype.

After you have completed your communion, you learn the arboreal language, which is spoken by most awakened trees. You must still use speak with plants to communicate with trees that are not awakened or do not understand arboreal.

You gain the ability to cast *one with plants* at will. If you cast *one with plants* near other trees, such that your root systems intertwine, you may spend 1 hour to gain vague impressions of what the nearby trees have experienced over the past 24 hours.

Once per day, you may cast *speak with plants*, except that it only works with trees. Most trees have a vague awareness of the actions of creatures around them, unless those creatures are actively threatening nearby trees. Some trees do take a particular interest in the affairs of creatures, however, and it is up to your GM to determine what kind of trees you encounter.

Spells @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.dileJ0Yxqg76LMvu]{One with Plants} (at will) | @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.qvwIwJ9QBihy8R0t]{Speak with Plants} (1/day, trees only)

Special You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Treespeaker archetype.

When you received the Call, it was as if something inside you was waiting for it, and you quickly became fluent in the language of trees. You are permanently under the effects of your speak with plants spell, instead of being able to cast it once per day. It still only works with trees. Because of your puissance with the language, you are able to draw out specific information even from trees that do not care about the affairs of creatures.

rules.text: Native Treespeaker You are permanently under the effects of speak with plants (trees only). You can draw out specific information even from disinterested trees.

description.value: You are able to expand your understanding of the natural world to other concepts and make connections with others in this way. When you attempt to Gather Information or Make an Impression you may use Nature instead of Diplomacy.

rules.text: Branch Out You can use Nature instead of Diplomacy to @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-macros.a471976e480ad8e2]{Gather Information} or @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-macros.ded86a6868dbb1a0]{Make an Impression}.

description.value: You can transform one or both of your arms into massive branches or trunks and use them as lethally as weapons. You gain the arboreal arms focus spell (page {arboreal-arms-focus-spell-page}). Increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1.

You draw upon the primal magic inherent in the world around you, and shape that magic into the powers granted to you by your arboreal patrons. You gain the Cast a Spell activity. You're trained in spell attack rolls and spell DCs. Your key spellcasting attribute for your Treespeaker archetype spells is your choice of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. You cast your Treespeaker spells spontaneously, and all of your Treespeaker spells are considered Signature Spells.

You gain two 3rd-rank spell slots that can only be used to cast the following spells, all of which are in your repertoire: protector tree, shillelagh, oaken resilience, entangling flora, fungal hyphae, shape wood, soothing blossoms, and splinter bolt (page {page-splinter-bolt-spell}).

When you rest you can sink little tendrils of roots into the ground and use the energy there to restore those around you. When you spend 10 minutes at rest or Refocusing, you and up to four allies gain fast

Effects Drag to ally: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.c473d62c45bf4d97]{Effect: Rejuvenate}

You gain one 4th-rank spell slot. You do not gain this slot if you have already gained a 4th-rank spell slot from the Treespeaker archetype. Add bloodspray curse, murderous vine, and petal storm to your repertoire.

You gain one 4th-rank spell slot. You do not gain this slot if you have already gained a 4th-rank spell slot from the Treespeaker archetype. Add soothing spring, rusting grasp, and vine shield (page {page-vine-shield-spell}) to your repertoire.

Each time you cast arboreal arms, you may choose agile, deadly d6, reach, or trip and add the chosen trait to the unarmed attack granted to you by arboreal arms. If you chose to transform both of your arms as part of the spell, you may choose a different trait for each arm.

You permanently take on some of the aspects of the hardy oak tree, whose timber is strong and hard, and whose seeds are nutritious to most humanoids. Your entire body becomes harder and stronger and your skin takes on a shade that gives away your arboreal influence.

You gain resistance to piercing and slashing damage equal to half your level.

When you successfully Shove or Grapple a creature, you may deal bludgeoning damage to them equal to your Constitution modifier.

You produce a number of acorns each day equal to half your level. Each of these acorns can be eaten with a single Interact action to restore a number of Hit Points equal to half your level.

Special You cannot select this feat if you have Aspect of Rosewood or Aspect of Yew.

You permanently take on some of the aspects of the iron rosewood tree, whose timber is rigid and unyielding, and whose oil is a fragrant perfume. Your skin takes on a shade that gives away your arboreal influence and becomes noticeably harder to the touch.

You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC, and to your DCs to avoid being shoved or grappled.

You gain resistance to physical damage equal to half your level.

You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks when interacting with someone who is close enough to smell you.

Special You cannot select this feat if you have Aspect of Oak or Aspect of Yew.

You permanently take on some of the aspects of the resilient yew tree, whose timber is both strong and springy, and whose seeds and leaves are extremely poisonous. Your entire body becomes more springy and absorbent to force.

You gain resistance to bludgeoning damage equal to half your level.

The first time you hit a creature with a ranged Strike on each turn, you gain a status bonus to damage equal to two times the number of weapon damage dice.

Additionally, your body produces a single dose of poison each day when you make your daily preparations. This may be any poison up to your level - 3 and it remains effective until you make your next daily preparations.

Special You cannot select this feat if you have Aspect of Oak or Aspect of Rosewood.

Your proficiency in spell attack rolls and spell DCs increases to expert. You gain an additional 3rd-rank spell slot. At 14th level, you gain one 5th-rank spell slot, and add plant form to your repertoire. At 16th level you gain one 6th-rank spell slot and add tangling creepers to your repertoire.

rules.text: Expert Arboreal Mystic Your spell attack rolls and spell DCs increase to expert. You also gain a 2nd-rank and 3rd-rank spell slot.

description.value:
From robust tree networks to tenuous connections of a few plants, nearly every plant creature across the face of Golarion is connected somehow. You have mastered submerging yourself and your allies into and through these connections. You can cast teleport once per day as an innate primal spell, but your destination must be adjacent to a tree within range. Because of your ability to communicate with trees as you travel, there is no chance for you to miss the intended destination.

Spells @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.69L70wKfGDY66Mk9]{Teleport} (1/day, primal, destination adjacent to a tree)

You can transform your legs into thick and powerful tree trunks. You gain the arboreal legs focus spell (page {arboreal-legs-focus-spell-page}). Increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. If you also have the arboreal arms focus spell, you may spend 2 actions instead of 1 when casting this focus spell. If you do so, you gain the effects of both spells at the cost of 1 focus point.

Targets the creature you have grappled

Requirements You have a creature grappled by your arboreal arm.

You have incredible control over the grappling tendrils of your arboreal arms and can use them to maintain a grapple with very little thought. You Grapple the target.

rules.text: Effortless Grapple You may Grapple as a free action (Flourish) to maintain a grapple with your arboreal arm.

description.value: You can transform your torso into a thick bole that protects you and offers nature's healing touch. You gain the arboreal bole focus spell (page {arboreal-heart-focus-spell-page}). Increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. If you also have the arboreal arms or arboreal legs focus spell, you may spend 2 or 3 actions instead of 1 when casting this focus spell. If you spend 2 actions, you gain the effects of this spell and one of the others at the cost of 1 focus point. If you spend 3 actions, you gain the effects of all 3 spells at the cost of 2 focus points.

You are so deeply respected by trees and arboreals that they and their other allies will answer your call freely to aid you. You may cast summon plant or fungus as an 8th-rank innate primal spell once per day. When you reach 18th level you cast this spell as a 9th-rank spell, and at 20th level you cast it as a 10th-rank spell.

Spells @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.jSRAyd57kd4WZ4yE]{Summon Plant or Fungus} (1/day, 8th rank; 9th at 18th, 10th at 20th)

Your proficiency in spell attack rolls and spell DCs increases to master. You gain a single 7th-rank spell slot and add regenerate to your repertoire. In addition to the normal targets of regenerate you may also target a living tree, regenerating any severed limbs, and causing the tree to blossom and bear fruit, depending on the type of tree. At 20th level, you gain one 8th-rank spell slot, and add forest avenger (page 31) to your repertoire. When you cast forest avenger, you may still cast your Treespeaker spells while in the battle form, though you cannot cast spells from any other source.

rules.text: Master Arboreal Mystic Your spell attack rolls and spell DCs increase to master. You also gain a 4th-rank spell slot.

description.value:
You fully embrace the arboreal aspect you chose. You gain the benefit below that is associated to the aspect you chose.

Aspect of Oak When you Grapple or Shove a creature, you may deal damage equal to four times your Constitution modifier (minimum 4).

Aspect of Rosewood The circumstance bonus to AC increases to +3.

Aspect of Yew The status bonus to damage applies to all of your ranged attacks.

value.value: one of Aspect of Oak, Aspect of Rosewood, or Aspect of Yew

description.value: You draw strength from the world around you when you have a few minutes to refocus. Once per day when you Refocus, you may also regain 2 expended Treespeaker spell slots of your choice.

Frequency once per day

The lower half of your body transforms into a tree trunk and roots race deep into the ground to draw healing energy. Your arms reach overhead and become a vast network of branches that extend out in every direction, instantly blossoming into a wild array of petals and pollen that showers the area around you in the life-giving power of nature. Each ally within 60 feet gains regeneration 30 for 3 rounds. This regeneration can be deactivated by fire damage. Each enemy within 60 feet takes 5d8 persistent vitality or persistent holy spirit damage, your choice. This activity has the vitality or holy and spirit traits based on this choice.

Effects Drag to ally: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.3f5714e2cd233e13]{Effect: Tree of Life}

You have been called to become a Treespeaker, but you have not yet answered the Call. Perhaps you do not feel that you are worthy, or you believe you have more pressing matters to attend to before answering the Call. Regardless, you have not refused the Call and you have begun to manifest some of the powers of a Treespeaker despite not yet having communed with an elder tree.

You become trained in Nature, or expert if you were already trained. You gain the ability to cast one with plants as an innate primal spell once per day.

You can sense the direction of the nearest elder tree at all times, and you can cast know the way as an innate primal cantrip at will.

Special You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Called Speaker archetype, though you may take the Treespeaker Dedication feat. It and any other feat from that archetype count as a Called Speaker feat for the purpose of satisfying this special requirement.

You haven't always resisted the Call, but every time you would instinctively answer it, something comes up from seemingly nowhere to stop you and keep you on your previous course. Once per day when you fail or critically fail a saving throw, you may reroll the triggering save with your highest saving throw modifier. You must use the new result.

rules.text: Dumb Luck Once per day, you may reroll a failed or critically failed saving throw using your highest save modifier. You must use the new result.

description.value:
Trigger You succeed on a saving throw against a mental effect.

You have endured the compulsion of the Call for a great period of time without answering it, demonstrating incredible powers of self-determination. You receive a critical success instead of a success on the triggering save.

Persuading others to see your point of view is a core requirement for any Treespeaker, but some excel above others. When you attempt a check to Make an Impression or Request, if the target is willing you may spend 10 minutes attempting the check instead of the usual amount of time. If you do, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your check. If the target is willing, you may spend 1 hour instead. If you do and roll a critical failure on the check, you receive a failure instead.

Your animal companion puts on a performance that people simply cannot look away from. Your animal companion spends 10 minutes performing and then attempts a Performance check to Perform. Compare the result of this check to the Will DC of each creature that can see or hear your animal companion.

Critical Success The creature is fascinated by your animal companion, and cannot look away for 10 minutes.

Success As critical success, but the creature will lose the fascinated condition if they hear a loud or concerning noise, such as the sounds of combat.

Failure The creature is unaffected by the performance.

Critical Failure The creature suspects that your animal companion's performance is cover for something else.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you Command it.

Distract [one-action] Targets A creature within 30 feet; Effect Your animal companion creates a distraction by performing stunts to get the target's attention. Your animal companion attempts a Performance check to Perform against the target's Will DC.

Critical Success The target is off-guard to your attacks until the end of your next turn.

Success The target is off-guard to your next attack.

Effects Drag to enemy: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.1b14a03f9994d355]{Effect: Distract (Off-Guard)}

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use if you succeed on a Nature check when you Command it. If you critically succeed on the check, the animal companion does not trigger enemy reactions at any time during this trick.

Fetch (manipulate, move) [two-actions] Targets an unattended object or an object held or worn by a willing ally; Effect Your animal companion Strides toward the target, Interacts to pick it up, and Strides toward you. If it reaches you, it can deliver the object into one of your hands if you have a free hand, otherwise it Releases the object after completing its Stride.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you Command it.

Open and Close (manipulate, move) [one-action] Your animal companion Strides up to its Speed and Interacts to open or close a door.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you Command it.

Play Dead [one-action] Your animal companion acts as though it just died. Your animal companion drops prone and attempts a Perform check against the Perception DC of all enemy observers. Each creature that the animal companion succeeds against believes the creature has died.

You specialize in teaching your animal companion to perform special tricks that they wouldn't normally be capable of. Choose one 4th-rank skill feat from this archetype that has the trick trait. You gain that skill feat, and your animal companion learns the accompanying trick; this feat doesn't count toward the 2 feats required before you can take another dedication feat. Unlike normal Commands that you give to your animal companion, tricks do not always automatically succeed. Each trick describes the checks that need to be made, if any. If a trick requires you to attempt a Nature check, do so when you grant your animal companion actions. If you succeed, you grant the actions as normal and your animal companion can use the trick in question if it has sufficient actions. If you fail, the animal companion still gains actions as normal, but it cannot use the trick this turn.

Your animal companion is a natural-born trickster and doesn't always wait for your direction before performing tricks. During an encounter, even if you don't use the Command an Animal action, your animal companion can still use one action on your turn that round to Stride or perform an action granted by a feat with the trick trait. You must still attempt any required Nature checks to Command an Animal if the trick normally requires it. Your animal companion can use this action to Burrow, Climb, Fly, or Swim instead of Stride if they have the corresponding movement type.

Choose one skill feat from this archetype that you qualify for and that has the trick trait. You gain that skill feat, and your animal companion learns the accompanying trick.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use if you succeed on a Nature check when you Command it. If you critically succeed on the check, the animal companion does not trigger enemy reactions at any time during this trick.

Deliver Consumable [two-actions] Your animal companion Strides up to its Speed to an ally within range of a single Stride, and Interacts to pour a potion into the ally's mouth or place a different type of consumable into the ally's hand. The animal companion must start this activity within melee reach of you, and you must be wearing or carrying the consumable that will be delivered.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you Command it.

Keep Away [two-actions] Requirements You animal companion is adjacent to you; Effect Your animal companion takes a defensive posture next to you, actively deflecting and disrupting incoming attacks. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC for 1 round. You lose this benefit if you are not adjacent to your animal companion at any time during this effect.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you succeed at a Nature check when you Command it.

Rolling Charge (move) [two-actions] Requirements No one is riding your animal companion, or you are riding them and you have the Trick Rider feat; Effect Your animal companion rolls into the enemies, either head over heels or on its side, attempting to bowl them over. They Stride up to twice their Speed in a straight line that can include enemies. Each time your animal companion would pass through an enemy's square, it attempts an Athletics check against the enemy's Reflex DC with the following outcomes. If you are riding your animal companion and you have the Trick Rider feat, you may remain mounted and you walk along your animal companion's back as they roll.

Success The enemy is knocked prone and moved 5 feet out of the path of your animal companion.

Failure Your animal companion fails to knock the enemy prone, but continues through its square.

Critical Failure Your animal companion stumbles to a halt in front of the enemy and does not finish their Stride.

You have trained with your animal companion extensively to move in tandem; mounting for a short stretch and then dismounting is all part of the performance. Once per minute when your animal companion is adjacent to you and uses an action with the move trait, you may choose to Mount them as a free action. When their movement ends you may Dismount as a free action. If your animal companion is your size or one size smaller you may still use this ability despite not normally being able to Mount your companion, but you must Dismount at the end of their movement.

In a great performance there is no room for a standard dismount: you perform an elegant flip, ride underneath your mount for a moment, or some other unbelievable trick. When you Dismount, you may attempt a Nature check and compare the result to the Will DC of each enemy creature adjacent to you at the end of your Dismount. Regardless of the outcome each creature is immune to your Flamboyant Dismount for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The creature is off-guard for 1 round.

Success The creature is off-guard until the end of your turn.
Your animal companion gains the ability to swim as though born to water. Your animal companion gains a swim Speed equal to its Speed.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you Command it.

Dazzling Display (auditory, visual) [two-actions] Your animal companion roars or demonstrates its prowess in a display of power that shakes enemies. Your animal companion attempts a Performance check to Perform. Compare the result to the Will DCs of each enemy creature within 20 feet of your animal companion with the following effects. Regardless of the outcome, each affected creature is immune to your animal companion's Dazzling Display for 24 hours.

Critical Success The enemy is frightened 2.

Success The enemy is frightened 1.

Failure The enemy is clumsy 1 until the end of your turn.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you Command it.

Drag (flourish, manipulate, move) [one-action] Targets an adjacent creature; Effect Your animal companion pulls an enemy into a more advantageous position, or drags an ally to safety. Your animal companion attempts an Athletics check against the Fortitude DC of the target with the following effects. If the target is willing or unconscious, treat the result as one degree higher.

Critical Success Your animal companion can drag the target a distance up to their Speed.

Success Your animal companion can drag the target a distance up to half their Speed.

Failure Your animal companion fails to move the target.

Critical Failure Your animal companion fails to move the target and falls prone.

You and your animal companion perform together whenever you're side-by-side. When you attempt a check using Deception, Diplomacy, Intimidation, or Performance and your animal companion is adjacent to you, your animal companion may attempt to Aid you without needing to prepare or spend a reaction. If your animal companion attempts one of these checks while you are adjacent to them, you may attempt to Aid them without needing to prepare or spend a reaction.

rules.text: Tandem Performance If your animal companion is adjacent, it can Aid you without preparing or spending a reaction.

description.value: Your animal companion gains the power of flight, either by growing wings or through some other supernatural power. Your animal companion gains a fly Speed equal to its Speed.

Your animal companion gains the following activity, which it can use when you succeed on a Nature check when you Command it.

Running Mount (move) [one-action] Targets a willing creature within the range of one Stride; Effect Your mount has mastered a trick of picking you or your allies up without help by sliding its head underneath of the target, or some other feat that is appropriate to the animal companion's anatomy. Your companion Strides and must end their movement adjacent to the target. The target may then Mount your animal companion immediately as a free action. Your companion Strides again.

You know you cannot let your target get away once you've found them, so you aim your attacks to slow them down. When you Strike a creature with a weapon or unarmed attack and critically succeed on the attack roll, you may choose to give the target a -10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until the end of your next turn. If you critically succeed on an attack roll to Strike a creature already under this effect, increase the circumstance penalty to Speeds by 5 feet. This is a critical specialization effect.

Effects Drag to enemy: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.6bd0f49f055bf010]{Effect: Hampering Critical}

rules.text: Hampering Critical On a critical hit, you may give the target a -10 ft. circumstance penalty to Speeds until end of your next turn. If already affected, increase the penalty by 5 ft.

description.value: The net gun is a difficult weapon to use, let alone master, but you have become proficient in it. You become trained in net guns and gain access to them. Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency with a weapon or unarmed strike, you also gain that proficiency with net guns.

rules.text: Net Gun Proficiency Your net gun proficiency scales with class features that grant expert or greater weapon proficiency.

description.value:
You are gifted in creating rough images that evoke a creature or person that you've seen. If you have something to draw with you may spend 10 minutes sketching a person or creature you have seen within the last 24 hours. Attempt a Society check; others may be able to identify the subject of the sketch based on the result, see below. Additionally you have the ability to better understand sketches from other artists. When you encounter a creature, you immediately know if you have seen a sketch of them before and where you saw it. If you see a sketch and you have already encountered the subject of the sketch, you remember who or what it was and where you saw them.

Success Anyone who has met or encountered the subject of the sketch immediately recognizes it.

Failure Anyone who has met or encountered the subject of the sketch can recognize them with a successful Society check against the standard DC for your level.

Critical Failure The sketch is completely unrecognizable.

You may not know everything about alchemy, but you have learned a very narrow set of concoctions to slow your enemies and make them easier to apprehend. You gain the basic alchemy benefits, though they only apply for soporifics: alchemical items with the sleep trait. You gain batches of infused reagents per day equal to your level. Your advanced alchemy level for creating these is your level - 3 (minimum 2). You learn the formula for Lethargy Poison (Player Core 2), and you gain access to all uncommon alchemical items with the sleep trait and their formulas. Additionally, you use your class DC or spell DC if it would be higher than the item's normal DC for your soporifics. For injury poisons, this effect only applies if you are the one attempting to Strike with the poisoned weapon.

Range 30 feet

Targets your hunted prey

Frequency once per hour

In life or death situations, sometimes you have to eliminate a target even if you're supposed to take them alive. You've learned that occasionally if you give them one last chance, you can still save them. You offer them a simple ultimatum: submit or be killed. The target must attempt a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. If the target has less than half their total Hit Points they take a -2 circumstance penalty to this save. Some of the effects of this ability refer to subduing the target. For the purpose of this effect, you may subdue the target with manacles or rope as a 2-action activity, but you must be wearing or carrying the manacles or rope. If you have the Hog Tie feat, this takes a single action instead of two. The target is also considered subdued if they gain the restrained condition through another means.

Critical Success The target rejects your ultimatum.

Success The target rejects your ultimatum, but fear of your retribution gives it a -1 status penalty to its attack rolls for 1 round.

Failure The target accepts your ultimatum and cannot take hostile actions until the end of your next turn. If you or an ally subdues the target during this effect with manacles or rope, it remains subdued and its disposition toward you changes from hostile to unfriendly. Otherwise it regains its senses and resumes hostilities after the end of your next turn.

Critical Failure The target accepts your ultimatum, falls prone, and waits to be subdued for 1 minute. This effect ends immediately if the creature takes any damage. The creature remains docile for 24 hours, after which it might attempt to escape or become hostile again at the GM's discretion.

Effects Drag to enemy: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.b148e793c91b6d8d]{Effect: Lethal Ultimatum}

Sometimes the people in an area aren't motivated to do the right thing just because it's right, and they need to be incentivized. You usually do pay them the agreed amount. You gain the Hireling Manager general feat. When you hire a hireling you treat their level as your level - 4 instead of 0, and they add their level to their skill checks. When you hire a hireling this way they have the minion trait. Additionally you may attempt a Deception check to Lie against a standard DC for your level. On a success, the hireling agrees that you will pay them "later" and is free for one week.

rules.text: Paid Posse You gain the Hireling Manager general feat. Your Posse hirelings can also use their trained skills to Aid you.

description.value: You tailor your soporifics to affect your targets more aggressively. Treat the level of your soporifics as your level for the purpose of the incapacitation trait and counteracting. If you critically hit a target and they must attempt a save against one of your soporifics, they take a -2 circumstance penalty to the save.

rules.text: Soporific Expert On a critical hit, targets take a -2 circumstance penalty to saves against your soporifics. Treat soporific level as your level for incapacitation/counteracting.

description.value: When you are giving guidance to your posse for how to hunt down your prey, you also lay the groundwork for how best to fight it. When you have a posse and you and your prey are both rolling initiative, the GM chooses a piece of information to give you from the following list: which of the enemy's weaknesses is highest, which of the enemy's saving throws has the lowest modifier, one immunity the enemy has, or which of the enemy's resistances is highest. The GM should choose the piece of information that will be most beneficial to the posse. You may share this information with your posse immediately as part of this effect.

rules.text: Tactical Posse When you and your prey both roll initiative and you have a Posse, the GM reveals one: highest weakness, lowest save modifier, one immunity, or highest resistance.

description.value:
You work together with the other members of your team to both corral and take down a target. When your hunted prey has the off-guard condition because you are flanking it with an ally, you and that ally gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Grapple, Shove, and Trip checks.

Effects Drag to ally: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.f4c1a20cf6af8dfc]{Effect: Dog Pile}

You can project your ultimatum to an entire group of enemies. When you use Lethal Ultimatum, you may choose to use it as a 2-action activity. If you do, it affects all enemies in a 30-foot emanation instead of a single target.

Targets The creature you successfully Grappled or Tripped

Requirements Your most recent action was a successful Grapple or Trip check, or you successfully used Opportunistic Grapple since your last turn. You must be wearing or carrying a length of rope.

After tripping or grabbing your target it's simple enough to tie them up for more convenient delivery. Attempt a Survival or Thievery check against the target's Fortitude DC. On a success, the target is restrained for 1 round or until they successfully Escape against the better of your Survival DC or Thievery DC. On subsequent turns you may repeat this action against the creature you have Hog Tied; if you succeed the restrained condition persists for an additional round. If you critically succeed on the initial check or any subsequent check, the restrained condition lasts for 1 minute, though the creature may still attempt to Escape against your Survival DC or Thievery DC as

You have mastered the use of the net gun as a legitimate primary weapon. When you are wielding a net gun, reduce its reload to 1. If you critically succeed on a Strike with a net gun, instead of the normal critical specialization you may cause the target to be grabbed by your net as if you had succeeded on a Grapple check with it. If you critically succeed on a Grapple check with your net gun, you may choose to also deal nonlethal damage as if you had hit the target with a Strike.

rules.text: Net Gun Specialist Net gun reload reduced to 1. On crit Strike: target is grabbed. On crit Athletics check to Grapple: also deal nonlethal Strike damage.

description.value: Your soporifics are truly works of art, lingering on the victim longer than normal. When a creature saves against one of your soporifics, they must attempt the save again at the start of their next turn. If they fail or critically fail that save, they are affected by the poison as normal.

rules.text: Persistent Soporifics When a creature saves against one of your soporifics, it must attempt the save again at the start of its next turn.

description.value: You are adept at getting the attention of a group of people and getting them to work together to take down a common enemy. When you roll initiative you may Hunt Prey on a creature you can see as a free action. If you do, you may immediately designate a Posse from nearby allies to assist you without needing to spend 1 minute giving guidance, and you all receive the circumstance bonus to your initiative rolls. If you have Tactical Posse, you also receive those benefits on the chosen target.

rules.text: Spontaneous Posse When you roll initiative, you may Hunt Prey as a free action and immediately designate a Posse (no 1-minute guidance needed).

description.value:
Your line of work often requires disabling opponents, and you have become a master of techniques that simplify that process. You Strike a creature. On a success, the target must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Regardless of the outcome, the target is immune to your Pressure-Point Strike for 24 hours. If the target falls unconscious as a result of the save, they fall prone, release what they're holding, and cannot attempt to make Perception checks to wake up until the duration ends. They also cannot be woken from nudging or shaking.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is stunned 1.

Failure The target falls unconscious for 1 round.

Critical Failure The target falls unconscious for 1 minute.

Requirements You have an adjacent creature grabbed or restrained

Sometimes disabling a target harmlessly is not an option. You snap the target's neck, or irrevocably damage them in some other way appropriate to their anatomy. This ability has no effect on a creature that is immune to precision damage. Attempt an attack roll against the target with a weapon or unarmed attack. As usual for effects with the incapacitation trait, reduce the degree of success of your attack roll by one step if the target's level is higher than yours. Regardless of the outcome, the target is immune to your Eliminate for 1 minute.

Critical Success The target is killed immediately as a death effect.

Success The target is drained 3.

Failure The target is drained 1.

You instinctively command respect and assistance from those around you, without any effort on your part. When you are in an encounter and you have a designated Posse, they are always working to Aid you. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to all attacks as if you had been successfully Aided until the end of the encounter.
No matter what your quarry does, you never give up on getting them trussed up for delivery. You are permanently quickened, and you can only use the extra action to Grapple, Trip, or Hog Tie.

rules.text: Implacable Captor You are permanently quickened. The extra action can only be used to Grapple, Trip, or Hog Tie.

description.value:
Tales of your captures and kills are widely known throughout every corner of Golarion; just by seeing you, enemies know their fate is sealed. When you roll initiative, each enemy creature that can see you must attempt a Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC with the following results.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is frightened 1.

Failure The creature is frightened 2.

Critical Failure The creature is frightened 3 and fleeing for 1 round.

rules.text: Legendary Bounty Hunter When you roll initiative, each enemy that can see you must attempt a Will save vs your class/spell DC. Success: frightened 1. Failure: frightened 2. Critical Failure: frightened 3 and fleeing 1 round.

description.value: As the primary person responsible for carrying on your culture's oral history, you have mastered quickly exchanging tales and stories to preserve the history of your people. When you spend 1 minute or more in conversation with a person who belongs to a particular town or group of people, you may attempt a Society check against the standard DC for your level as you exchange the tales from your respective areas or groups. On a success, you become trained in the Lore of that town or group of people for 24 hours. For example you might gain Whispering Way Lore if you use this ability when conversing with a cultist of the Whispering Way. The Lore you learn from this should always be a specific Lore, and the DC for any checks made with it should be adjusted accordingly. If you are master in Society, you become expert in the new Lore instead of trained.

You think back to a vast trove of stories that represent a diverse array of axioms and truths, and try to find the one story that perfectly resonates with the moment. You exhort an ally with this tale to bolster their efforts. This action counts as sufficient preparation to Aid that ally. When you use the Aid reaction to help that ally, you can roll Recall Knowledge in place of the usual check, using a skill that has the Recall Knowledge action and is fitting for the situation. This should commonly be a Society check to remember stories of what other humanoids have experienced, but you may use other skills with the Recall Knowledge action, including Lore skills, at the GM's discretion. If you use a Lore skill, reduce the DC of the Aid check based on whether the Lore skill is specific or unspecific as normal for Recalling Knowledge.

Range 60 feet

Targets the hero of your Spin Tale

Requirements Spin Tale is active

Frequency once per day

The illusions that spring up as you spin your tale are far from harmless, serving to confuse the villain about who the hero really is. You cast mirror image but instead of targeting yourself, it affects the hero of your tale. If your tale has multiple heroes, choose one to receive this benefit.

Spells @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.j8vIoIEWElvpwkcI]{Mirror Image} (targets tale hero, not self)

Requirements Spin Tale is active

A great story doesn't just have a hero and a villain, it also has someone that challenges the hero's journey and highlights their heroic qualities: the foil. The foil can be a friend of the hero who unintentionally makes their task more challenging, or it can be a rival. Choose a creature within 60 feet. If the target is an enemy, the hero gains the same benefits against the foil that he gains against the villain. If the target is an ally, the hero gains a +1 status bonus on checks to Aid the target, and each time the target takes damage, reduce that damage by 5 and the hero takes 5 damage. This damage cannot be reduced in any way. If you are master in Performance, increase the damage transferred to 10. If you are legendary, increase it to 15. This effect remains active as long as the effect of Spin Tale is active.

Effects Enemy: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.e92b9bdf0db0e7e9]{Establish Foil} | Ally: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.fa93b46a79999283]{Establish Foil (Ally)}

Requirements Spin Tale is active

The hero of the story has experienced a setback, and digs deeper to press forward. They are willing to go further than before, and possibly even cross boundaries they normally wouldn't. Attempt a Performance check against a Standard DC of your level. On a success, as long as your story continues, the hero gains a status bonus to damage rolls equal to the number of weapon damage dice of the unarmed attack or weapon they are wielding that has the most weapon damage dice. When they critically hit an enemy with a Strike, the enemy is frightened 1. If you have previously lightened the tone of the story with Lighten Tone, that effect ends. If your story has multiple heroes, they each gain this effect.

Effects Drag to hero: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.465a1877f36546c4]{Effect: Darken Tone}

Requirements Spin Tale is active

The hero of the story is triumphantly pressing forward. They have momentum on their side and they feel unstoppable. Attempt a Performance check against a Standard DC of your level. On a success, the hero gains temporary Hit Points equal to half your level. Once per round when the hero damages the villain, they gain these temporary Hit Points again. This effect lasts as long as your story continues. If you have previously darkened the tone of the story with Darken Tone, that effect ends. If your story has multiple heroes, they each gain this effect.

Effects Drag to hero: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.7b1a8673d2df148c]{Effect: Lighten Tone}

Frequency once per day

Sometimes the story requires that the hero or one of their allies loses a decisive battle, only to come back stronger later. Someone has to provide succor to the hero in this time and serve as the wise guide who sets them on the right path. You cast soothe on an ally, heightened to half your level.

Spells @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.szIyEsvihc5e1w8n]{Soothe} (1/day, heightened to half your level)

Requirements Spin Tale is active and you haven't used Second Act this encounter

As the story enters the second act, the folklorist emphasizes and builds the confrontation between the hero and the villain. The villain must attempt a Will save against your Performance DC with the following outcomes. If your story has multiple heroes, they are all affected by the outcome. If your story has multiple villains, choose one to target with this effect.

Critical Success The hero is off-guard to the villain for 1 round.

Success The villain is off-guard to the hero for 1 round.

Failure The villain is off-guard to the hero for 1 minute.

Critical Failure As failure, and the villain gains weakness 3 to the damage type of the hero's primary weapon. If the hero does not typically use a weapon, choose between bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing.

Effects @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.eb0211e6e2a91446]{Off-Guard 1 rnd} | @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.d796bf5da57e9dee]{Off-Guard 1 min} | @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.b875ad4c663e23b7]{Off-Guard + Weakness}

Many stories don't revolve around a single villain, but instead contain several. When you use Spin Tale, all enemies within 60 feet are the villains of the story.

If you have Establish Foil, you may now target an enemy who is already a villain with that ability. If you do, the target must attempt a Will save against your Performance DC. On a failure it takes a -2 status penalty to all attack rolls and skill checks against targets that are not the hero of your story. If your story has multiple heroes, choose one to be the hero for this effect. This effect lasts as long as your Spin Tale is active.

Effects Drag to foil: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.b7e64737c5fa8b5b]{Effect: Cast of Villains Foil}

Requirements Spin Tale is active and you have used Second Act in the current story

Frequency once per hour

As the story enters the third act, the folklorist describes the final confrontation between the hero and the villain, with the hero bringing all of their energy to bear, including the help of their allies. The villain must attempt a Will save against your Performance DC.

Critical Success The villain is unaffected

Success The villain is off-guard for 1 round.

Failure As success, and the status bonus from Spin Tale increases to +2 as long as your tale continues.

Critical Failure As failure, and the villain gains weakness to the damage type of the hero's primary weapon equal to half your level. If the hero does not typically use a weapon, choose between bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing.

Effects @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.fb02e9fd8885f9aa]{Off-Guard 1 rnd} | @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.15e990cb1e0603e9]{Enhanced Tale} | @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.e668caa1ba2252e7]{Off-Guard + Weakness}

Frequency once per 10 minutes

After a difficult fight, you soothe your companions and prepare them for their next stories. When you're not in an encounter you may spend 1 round soothing up to 5 allies. Each ally regains Hit Points equal to four times your level.

Requirements Spin Tale is active

The hero presses their advantage to defeat the villain once and for all. The hero of the story immediately takes an additional turn, and receives a +3 status bonus to all checks during this turn. If your story has multiple heroes, choose one ally who is receiving the hero's benefits to take this additional turn. After the hero has taken this extra turn the story has ended, and you cannot Spin Tale again during this encounter. The hero's additional turn does not change their initiative, and they still act on their regular turn as normal.

Effects Drag to hero: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.13c03c476020040e]{Effect: Climax}
You can use your skill in tracking to find elusive foes. When you Seek an area looking for your hunted prey, you may roll Survival instead of Perception.

rules.text: Instant Tracker You can use Survival instead of Perception to @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-macros.5c9c6dc5fe2fc44b]{Seek} your hunted prey.

description.value: You can detect the presence of certain types of creatures with almost no information to work from. As a 10-minute activity you can use inscrutable techniques, such as listening to the wind, smelling for certain signs, or noticing the way certain foliage is damaged to sense nearby creatures. Choose a type of creature that is a valid target for your Hunt Prey ability and against whom your Stealth bonus and Speed reduction apply. For example, you may normally choose between animals, beasts, and dragons. If you have It's All Game, you may choose from any type of creature. You automatically know whether any creatures of the chosen type are within 1 mile of you. You do not know the direction of any creatures that you detect, but you do gain sufficient information to begin Tracking the creatures in exploration. The GM may call for additional Survival checks to determine if you know which specific creature you are Tracking if there are more than one.

After a successful hunt, you sometimes collect a trophy from your prey to honor it and enshrine your victory. This trophy serves as a constant reminder of the hunt and your prey. If you used Hunt Prey in an encounter and the target of that ability was killed, you may spend 10 minutes collecting a trophy from the creature's body. At the GM's discretion, collecting trophies from certain creatures, such as creatures from your own ancestry or similar ancestries, may violate the edicts or anathemas of your deity, cause, or ancestry. Keep track of the creature types you have collected trophies from; as long as you display your trophies visibly, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks against and attempts to Recall Knowledge about creatures of the same type (e.g. beast, dragon, fiend, etc). If you are a master of Survival, this bonus increases to +2.

rules.text: Trophy Collector This bonus increases to +2 if you are a master of Survival. You also gain this bonus to Recall Knowledge about creatures of the same type.

description.value: You can keep the pressure up on your target to prevent them from escaping. When you apply your Speed reduction to a target by striking your Hunted Prey while it's off-guard the target also gains the drained 1 condition, and the creature no longer becomes immune to your Speed reduction afterwards.

rules.text: Improved Slowing Strikes When applying Speed reduction to off-guard hunted prey, the target also gains drained 1. The target no longer becomes immune to your Speed reduction.

description.value: You have learned to hunt men and monsters just as much as beasts and dragons. You may now designate any type of creature as prey, instead of only animals, beasts, and dragons. You gain the Stealth bonus and Speed reduction from the Game Hunter Dedication when you hunt any prey. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the DC for your Speed reduction effect as well.

rules.text: It's All Game Your Speed reduction DC gains a +1 circumstance bonus. You may now Hunt Prey against any creature type.

description.value: Timing is everything, and patience is the key to good timing. If you choose to Delay on your first turn of an encounter, you may immediately Hunt Prey as a free action, but you may only target a creature that hasn't acted yet. If you do so, you must wait until the target of your Hunt Prey has ended their turn before you can rejoin the initiative. When you do, you deal an additional 1d6 precision damage the first time you successfully Strike your prey this turn. This damage increases to 2d6 at level 16. If you are also undetected by the target of your Hunt Prey, increase this damage to 1d10, or 2d10 at level 16. If you have the rogue's Surprise Attack feature (or similar ability) and its effect would apply at the point when you Delay, you still gain its effects despite acting after the target creature.

rules.text: Lie in Wait If you Delay on your first turn, you may Hunt Prey as a free action. First Strike deals +1d6 precision (+2d6 at 16th; +1d10/+2d10 if undetected).

description.value: You know it's always a risk that the hunter may become the hunted. Despite that, you have weathered many hunts and come out the victor, even when your prey got in a lucky hit. When your hunted prey critically hits you, you may attempt a DC 17 flat check. On a success, it becomes a normal hit. Alternatively you may choose to automatically reduce the critical hit to a normal hit; if you do, you cannot use this ability again until after your next daily preparations.

rules.text: Hunter's Verve When your hunted prey critically hits you, attempt a DC 17 flat check to reduce it to a normal hit. Or auto-reduce once per day.

description.value: You set your traps so precisely that only one creature could ever reasonably trigger them. When you set a snare you may designate a target creature that you can see, that you're aware of, or that you have successfully Tracked. The snare can only be triggered by that creature. If the snare triggers and would affect that creature, the snare receives a +3 circumstance bonus to attack rolls, DCs, and any other checks the snare attempts.

Targets a creature whose type matches a trophy you've collected

Frequency once per minute

You have grown accustomed to instilling fear in your foes with your trophies, and using them as totems to trigger memories about all the creatures you have hunted. You attempt to Demoralize or Recall Knowledge about the target. When you Demoralize this way it loses the auditory trait and gains the visual trait. You do not take a penalty for not sharing a language with the target.

When you hunt a target, it's not possible for them to act more quickly than you do. Once per day when you roll initiative and you have already used Hunt Prey on a creature that is in the current encounter, you may choose to go right before your hunted prey. Adjust your initiative score to be one higher than the target.

rules.text: Hunter's Certainty Once per day, if you've already Hunted Prey on a creature in this encounter, you may adjust your initiative to be 1 higher than your prey's.

description.value:
Targets your hunted prey

Requirements You are wielding a piercing or slashing melee weapon

You relentlessly attack the same part of your prey's body, opening up an ever-widening hemorrhage so that even if your prey escapes you, it will still perish from its wounds. You Strike your target with the required weapon, and on a hit the target takes 2d6 persistent bleed damage. If the target was already suffering from persistent bleed damage from an effect other than Lacerating Strikes, you increase that damage by 2d6.

Once per round on subsequent rounds, if you hit the target with the required weapon and it is still bleeding, increase the persistent bleed damage by 1d6. If the target's persistent bleed condition ends for any reason, this effect also ends. If you hit the target with the required weapon and it is already suffering from 4d6 bleed damage from your Lacerating Strikes, its persistent bleed condition ends and it immediately takes 10d10 damage of the same type as your weapon and it is immune to your Lacerating Strikes for 24 hours.

Targets your hunted prey

Requirements You are wielding a ranged weapon

You mercilessly attack a part of your prey's body that is responsible for movement, reducing their mobility with each successive shot. You Strike your target with the required weapon, and on a success the target is clumsy 1 until the end of your next turn and marked for as long as they remain clumsy from this ability.

Once per round on subsequent rounds when you successfully hit your marked prey you extend the duration of their clumsy condition to the end of your next turn and increase its value by 1. This does not increase the target's clumsy condition if it came from a different source. If you hit the target while this ability is active and they are clumsy 3, they instead become stunned for 1 round. Once the target is stunned it is no longer marked by your Slowing Shots and is immune to this ability for 24 hours.

You can catch the trail of any creature, whether there is a trail or not. You may Track any creature on the same plane, no matter the distance, by declaring their name or describing them. For example you might Track "the nearest bear," or "Bendrion Pelonius," or "the creature who destroyed this village". You do not need tracks or physical phemonema to follow, you just sense their direction intuitively. As normal you may Hunt Prey on any creature you are Tracking in exploration, including those you are able to track with this feat.

When you are hunting a creature, you rise to the demands of the moment, moving faster and more certainly than you ever thought possible. When you Track a creature in exploration, increase your travel Speed to match that of your target. When you are in an encounter and you have used Hunt Prey, all of your Speeds are equal to the highest Speed that either you or your hunted prey has.

rules.text: Unshakeable Hunter When hunting prey, all your Speeds equal the highest Speed between you and your prey.

description.value: You can apply your natural medicines even in the midst of combat. Attempt a Nature check with the same DC as for Treat Wounds and restore the corresponding amount of HP; this doesn't remove the wounded condition. As with Treat Wounds, you can attempt checks against higher DCs if you have the minimum proficiency rank in Nature that would normally require Medicine. The target is then temporarily immune to Battle Herbs for 1 day. If you have Fresh Ingredients, you can spend a batch of herbs to gain a +2 circumstance bonus to this check.

You are always on the lookout for useful herbs for your potions and poultices. When you use the Search exploration activity and spend at least 1 hour searching you may also attempt a Nature check with the following effects. The GM should use a level-based DC, adjusted by how rare useful ingredients might be in an area. If it would be unreasonable for you to find any useful herbs, mosses, flowers, or other natural ingredients the GM may make this unavailable in certain areas. You cannot exceed your initial daily allotment of infused reagents by gathering this way. Infused reagents gathered this way may only be used to create items specified in the Herbalist archetype.

Critical Success You find enough materials to gain 2 infused reagents, and you may make items from these 2 infused reagents immediately.

Success You find enough materials to gain 1 infused reagent, and you may spend an additional 10 minutes to make items from it.

Critical Failure You do not find anything and your search for ingredients has distracted you from your original purpose. You critically fail the Search activity that triggered this check.

Adventuring is often stressful and it can be difficult to rest easy. You can brew an herbal tea that contains a mix of soothing and relaxing ingredients that ensure you and your allies get a good rest before making your daily preparations. Each time you and up to 5 allies rest and make your daily preparations, you each receive temporary Hit Points equal to half your level. These temporary Hit Points last until your next daily preparations. If you're master in Nature, you each receive temporary Hit Points equal to your level instead.

Effects Drag to ally: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.87a3025666e9ef20]{Effect: Evening Brew}

Targets an adjacent ally

You can put together of small bundle of herbs that enhance concentration and stave off the effects of weariness for an ally to chew on. You expend 1 batch of infused reagents. For 1 hour the target's fatigued condition and its effects are suppressed. After the hour has passed, if the source of the fatigue is still present, the target is affected by the condition as normal. During this hour the target also gains a +1 status bonus to Acrobatics, Athletics, and Perception checks.

Effects Drag to target: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.f9997f6e3ab3f45d]{Effect: Energizing Chew}
You're always ready to pull out the necessary poultice at a moment's notice. You Interact to draw a poultice that you're wearing.

You restrict your diet to a very small list of specific foods to prepare your body's immune system to be in peak condition for your next adventure. When you spend at least 1 week of downtime that doesn't involve much strenuous activity, you (and up to five allies you can convince to join you) spend that week eating only this restrictive list of foods. For 1 week after you use this ability, you and any allies who participated in the cleanse gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude saves.

Effects Drag to ally: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.fae091f3ecb8de70]{Effect: Dietary Cleanse}

Over time you get to know your frequent patients better and are able to personalize your ministrations to maximize their effectiveness. When you Treat Disease or Treat Poison, you may continue attempting the check after the first attempt, replacing the results of the initial check with your most recent attempt. If you succeed on a check to Treat Disease or Treat Poison, and the target rolls a critical failure on their next save against that effect, they receive a failure instead.

rules.text: Long-Term Care You may continue retrying Treat Disease/Treat Poison. On success, the target's next critical failure on their save becomes a failure instead.

description.value: When you create an herbal elixir, you can prepare it as a vapor bomb instead. When you Craft an item with the elixir trait using infused reagents, you may prepare it as a vapor bomb instead. A vapor bomb loses the elixir and ingested trait and gains the inhaled trait. A vapor bomb can be activated and thrown by Interacting with one hand. The vapor bomb creates a 10-foot burst within 20 feet. Each creature within the burst receives the effect of the herbal elixir. If the elixir has a variable numerical effect, such as the amount of healing granted by an Elixir of Life, each creature in the burst receives half the normal benefit from the variable effect.

You can prepare a tincture to help someone shrug off the effects of pain. As a 10-minute activity you can expend 2 batches of infused reagents to prepare a tincture for a specific creature, taking into account the creature's size, weight, ancestry, and other factors. Attempt a Nature check with a Hard DC based on the target's level. The target creature must consume the tincture immediately as part of this activity or it spoils.

Critical Success For 24 hours, the next time the target creature would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not immediately killed, they instead remain at 1 Hit Point, their wounded condition increases by 1, and they gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level for 1 minute.

Success As critical success, but the duration is 1 hour instead of 24 hours.

Failure You fail to calculate the dosage correctly, but realize your mistake before it's too late. The target is unaffected, and you do not expend your infused reagents.

Critical Failure The target immediately falls unconscious and stays that way for 1 hour. They cannot be roused by any means. The infused reagents used to prepare this tincture are lost.

Effects Drag to target: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.63bf10a97fbf06c9]{Effect: Anesthetic Tincture}

When you create healing vapor bombs, you add a pungent odor that helps break your allies out of a stupor. When you use a vapor bomb, it also reduces the stupefied condition of each creature in the area by 1. If any creature in the area has the confused or controlled condition, you may attempt a Nature check to counteract those conditions. Make a separate counteract check for each creature. If you apply Smelling Vapors to a Vapor Preparation of a Bottled Catharsis elixir, all creatures in the area instead automatically lose their stupefied condition, and you attempt to counteract the affected creatures' confused or controlled conditions using the higher of your Nature or the elixir's counteract modifier.

It may not be your proudest piece of knowledge, but in your time studying herbalism you have learned ways to ease people into the next life to reduce their suffering. When you create items as part of your daily preparations, you may spend 3 infused reagents to create one dose of Tears of Death (Player Core 2). It remains viable until your next daily preparations.

You have mastered a combination of herbs that restores someone to perfect health. Each day when you make your daily preparations you create one dose of true panacea, a potion that is effective until you make your next daily preparations. When a creature drinks your True Panacea, their Hit Points are restored to maximum. Attempt a single Nature check to counteract all negative conditions currently affecting the target. Compare the result of the check to each condition's counteract level and DC.

Your knowledge of your favored terrain allows you to assemble devastating hazards at a moment's notice with almost no materials. You carry enough knickknacks with you to construct four snares when combined with the natural materials around you. The first four snares you craft in your favored terrain each day that don't have the clockwork trait don't cost any resources to Craft, and you may deploy them with 3 Interact actions if they would normally take 1 minute to Craft. The number of snares you can Craft this way increases to six if you're a master in Crafting and eight if you're legendary.

Trigger An ally within 30 feet of you is about to attempt a skill check to move through terrain or a saving throw against a hazard in your favored terrain.

You quickly point out a hazard or a better way to navigate. For the rest of their turn, your ally gains a +2 circumstance bonus to skill checks to move within your favored terrain, such as an Acrobatics check to Balance or a Stealth check to Sneak. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus on saving throws against hazards (including the triggering hazard, and they can ignore the effects of non-magical difficult terrain. This action gains the auditory or visual trait based on how you convey the information to your ally.

Effects Drag to ally: @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-effects.d7db6b01b4be17a6]{Effect: Terrain Guidance}

You have traveled beyond just one domain and have mastered lands far and wide. Choose another type of terrain from your Favored Terrain feat that you have not yet chosen. You gain all of your Horizon Walker benefits in this terrain in addition to the first terrain you chose.

If you have Terrain Master, you may choose which of your favored terrains to replace for the purpose of that feat.

Special You may select this feat more than once. Each time you must select a terrain you have not yet chosen.

You have encyclopedic knowledge about the types of creatures that can be found in your favored terrain. When you are in your favored terrain and you attempt a Recall Knowledge check to identify a creature, you may roll Survival instead of the normal skill.

rules.text: Known Predators In your favored terrain, you may use Survival instead of the normal skill to @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-macros.523adc6362f06afd]{Recall Knowledge} to identify a creature.

description.value: You have hunted and gathered in your favored terrain so long that it has become second nature to you. When you Subsist in your favored terrain, increase the degree of success of your Survival check by one step. For example, if you roll a success, you receive a critical success instead. If you roll a failure, you receive a success, and so forth.

You have completely mastered one of your favored terrains, and have gained an edge that helps you no matter which terrain you find yourself in. Choose a terrain type that is one of your favored terrains. You gain the benefit associated to that terrain type permanently. If you also have the Sure Foot feat, increase its circumstance bonus to +3 when you are in the chosen terrain.

  • Aquatic You gain a +2 status bonus to Athletics checks to Swim.
  • Arctic You gain resistance to cold equal to your level.
  • Desert You are immune to the fatigued condition.
  • Forest You gain a +1 status bonus to Stealth checks.
  • Mountain You gain a +2 status bonus to Athletics checks to Climb.
  • Underground You gain low-light vision, or darkvision if you already have low-light vision. When attempting a flat check to target a hidden or concealed creature, reduce the DC by 1.
  • Plains Reduce your Speed penalty from armor by 5 feet.
  • Sky You gain resistance to electricity equal to your level.
  • Swamp You gain resistance to poison equal to your level.

Special You can take this feat more than once. Each time you must choose a different terrain that is already a favored terrain for you.

rules.text: Terrain Specialist (Underground) You gain low-light vision, or darkvision if you already have low-light vision. Reduce the flat check DC to target hidden or concealed creatures by 1.
Terrain Specialist (Plains) Reduce your Speed penalty from armor by 5 feet.

description.value: Because you rely on the terrain around you to construct your snares, you can easily expand them to create larger hazards. When you Craft a snare without paying a cost as part of your Natural Snares feat, you may choose to have the snare occupy a 10-foot square instead of a 5-foot square.

Some of the greatest horizon walkers learn the mysteries of the planes, and master travel in those realms as well. When selecting terrains from your Favored Terrain feat, you may also choose from the elemental planes (Air, Earth, Fire, and Water) and the Astral Plane, Ethereal Plane, or Netherworld. You must still meet the travel prerequisite of this feat to do so. When you gain this feat, choose one of these planes; that plane becomes a favored terrain for you. You gain a benefit based on your choice from the list below, which counts as having the Terrain Specialist feat for that type of terrain. If you take the Terrain Specialist feat in the future, you may choose from these options in addition to those listed in that feat's description.

  • Astral Plane You can cast translocate once per day as an innate primal spell.
  • Ethereal Plane Once per day as a reaction when you are hit by an attack, you may shift into the ethereal plane momentarily; the attack misses you instead, or becomes a regular hit if it would have been a critical hit. If you are in the ethereal plane already, you shift to the material plane instead.
  • Plane of Air You can cast fly once per day as an innate primal spell.
  • Plane of Earth You gain imprecise tremorsense with a range of 30-feet.
  • Plane of Fire You gain fire resistance equal to your level.
  • Plane of Water You gain the ability to breathe underwater.
  • Netherworld You can cast invisibility as a 4th-rank innate primal spell once per day.

interplanar teleport and other accomodations required to visit the chosen plane. After this downtime is complete, they qualify to take this feat.

Special This feat requires having spent 30 days on the chosen plane. At GM discretion, 30 days of downtime and 1,000 gp may substitute.

Although horizon walkers always begin their journey in the wilderness, an uncommon few of them adapt their skills to urban environments. You gain urban areas as a favored terrain, which includes cities, towns, and villages. Regardless of where you are, you may cast 4th-rank illusory disguise once per day as an innate primal spell. This counts as having the Terrain Specialist feat for the urban favored terrain. If you have the ranger Wild Stride feature, you may roll Survival instead of Diplomacy when attempting to Gather Information or Make an Impression while in an urban area.

rules.text: Urban Walker In urban areas with Wild Stride, use Survival instead of Diplomacy to @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-macros.17df8cb7c1d6ea7f]{Gather Information} or @UUID[Compendium.everything-archetypes-wilderness.eaw-macros.e4974a98cbd37d2c]{Make an Impression}.

description.value:
You have traveled to one of the outer planes of existence; you have adapted to travel in that realm and learned to fight its denizens. Choose one of the outer planes listed below. You treat that plane as favored terrain. Your Strikes deal an extra 1d6 damage of the type associated with the chosen plane. You may choose other outer planes at your GM's discretion, who should assign a damage type that is thematically appropriate.

  • Abaddon void damage
  • Abyss poison damage
  • Axis force damage
  • Elysium sonic damage
  • Heaven spirit damage
  • Hell fire damage
  • Nirvana vitality damage
  • The Maelstrom mental damage

interplanar teleport and other accomodations required to visit the chosen plane. After this downtime is complete, they qualify to take this feat.

Special You may choose this feat more than once, selecting a different outer plane each time. This feat requires having spent 30 days on the chosen plane.

In your experience the wilderness is rarely merciful, and you have taken lessons from nature's own designs to empower your snares. When you Craft a snare without paying a cost as part of your Natural Snares feat, you may choose an additional effect that applies if the snare successfully hits a creature or if the creature fails its saving throw against the snare's effect. Choose from 2d6 persistent bleed damage, 4d6 slashing damage, or immobilized for 1 round.

You have expanded your magical tricks to use the terrain around you to your advantage. You can cast flammable fumes, safe passage, and solid fog as innate primal spells, each once per day.

You are peerless in your favored terrain. Choose a terrain type for which you have the Terrain Specialist feat. You gain the benefit associated to that terrain type permanently.

  • Aquatic You ignore difficult terrain while swimming, including when moving up or down.
  • Arctic When you gain the slowed condition, reduce its value by 1.
  • Astral Plane You may cast translocate twice per day instead of once per day, and as a 5th-rank spell, instead of 4th-rank.
  • Desert You are immune to the dazzled condition.
  • Ethereal Plane You can cast ethereal jaunt once per day as an innate primal spell.
  • Forest You can attempt to Hide or Sneak anywhere, without needing cover or concealment. When you attempt a check to Hide or Sneak this way, you have a -2 circumstance penalty to your Stealth check.
  • Mountain You may gain or Dismiss the benefits of gecko grip at will as a free action. Your climb Speed while under this effect is equal to double your Speed.
  • Plane of Air You learn Sussuran and you may cast fly at will instead of once per day.
  • Plane of Earth You learn Petran and gain a burrow Speed equal to your Speed.
  • Plane of Fire You learn Pyric and can cast fireball once per day heightened to half your level as an innate primal spell.
  • Plane of Water You learn Thalassic and can speak telepathically to creatures with the amphibious, aquatic, or water trait as long as they are within 500 feet. You can communicate this way even with creatures that don't speak any languages.
  • Underground You gain greater darkvision. Reduce the DC for flat checks to target concealed or hidden creatures by 2 instead of 1.
  • Plains You ignore all Speed penalties from armor, encumbrance, and non-magical effects.
  • Netherworld You can cast umbral journey once per day as an innate primal spell. For you the cast time is 2 actions instead of 1 minute.
  • Sky You ignore difficult terrain while Flying, including when moving upward.
  • Swamp When you attempt a save against a poison effect, improve the result of the save by one step.
  • Urban You can roll Survival instead of Diplomacy for any check that would normally use Diplomacy.

Special You can take this feat more than once. Each time you must choose a different terrain for which you have Terrain Specialist.

You have learned to tap into the true power of the land. You can cast nature's reprisal as a 9th-rank spell once per day.

Requirements You are wielding a ranged weapon with reload 1 or less, and you have not made any Strikes yet this encounter.

You launch a quick barrage of arrows at the start of the fight that breaks your opponent's courage. Attempt to Demoralize an enemy within your weapon's first range increment, then make two Strikes against that target with the required weapon. This Demoralize loses the auditory trait and you don't take a penalty if you don't share a language with the target. If both Strikes hit, increase the target's frightened condition by 1 (to frightened 1 if it does not have the frightened condition). If your weapon requires an action to reload, this ability is a 3-action activity.

The best scouts see the enemy without being seen. Whenever you use the Scout activity, you may also Avoid Notice at the same time without further reductions to your Speed. You gain the Foil Senses skill feat even if you do not meet the prerequisites.

rules.text: Hidden Scout You gain the Foil Senses skill feat even if you don't meet the prerequisites.

description.value: No matter how careful you are, if you are scouting ahead long enough you will eventually get caught. When that happens, the best thing you can do is move faster than whoever spotted you. When you roll initiative because you failed a Stealth check, or because an enemy succeeded on a Perception check against your Stealth DC, you gain a +2 status bonus to your initiative roll. If you tie with an enemy's initiative roll, you act before they do.

Requirements You are hidden from or undetected by the target

You make a single attack from hiding and immediately hide again, staying on the move to keep the enemy in constant confusion about your position. You Strike and then Sneak. If you succeed on your Sneak check and you were undetected before using Beleaguering Attack, you remain undetected.

When you spot an enemy, you make note of details that you can report back to your allies; even if you don't know about the target, they may. When you see a creature you may retain specific information about their appearance for 10 minutes. During that time, you may relay this information to your allies. After hearing about the creature, your allies can attempt to Recall Knowledge about the creature as though they were seeing it themselves. If you are an expert in Perception, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to this check. If you are master the bonus is +2, and if you are legendary it is +3. If you choose to retain specific information about another creature during this time, you forget important details about the first and cannot accurately explain it until you've seen it again.
