rules.text: Detailed Report Retain a creature's details for 10 min. Allies who hear your report get +1 (expert), +2 (master), or +3 (legendary Perception) circumstance bonus to Recall Knowledge about it.

description.value: You have developed a series of signs and signals with your allies that allow you to clandestinely communicate with them in a variety of situations. You can communicate through hand gestures, sounds of creatures native to an area, or even more contrived scenarios. Attempt a Survival check against a level-based DC, adjusted for how contrived the communication is. On a success, your allies understand your message clearly. Compare the result of your Survival check to the Perception DCs of any enemies that would be

Requirements You are wielding a ranged weapon

After scouting an enemy position, you know who needs to be dealt with first; you fire a shot to mark the target that your allies should focus their attention on. Make a Strike with the required weapon. If the Strike hits, the next creature other than you to attack the same target gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their roll, or a +2 circumstance bonus if your Strike was a critical hit.

When you are able to scout out the enemy position, you can prepare yourself for the combat ahead. When you have used the Scout exploration activity, and would gain the circumstance bonus to your initiative roll you may always choose to roll Stealth for your initiative instead of Perception. When you do so, if your initiative result is higher than that of all enemy combatants you may immediately use a single action.

rules.text: Scout's Ambush When you used the Scout exploration activity and gain a circumstance bonus to initiative, you may roll Stealth instead of Perception. If your initiative beats all enemies, you may immediately use a single action.

description.value: With practice you are no longer limited to describing a single creature in great detail. Now you can retain specific information about any number of creatures you have seen, and you may relay those details to someone at any time before your next daily preparations. When someone succeeds at a Recall Knowledge check using your information from Exhaustive Report, they gain additional information or context.

rules.text: Exhaustive Report You can retain details about any number of creatures (not just one). Relay details at any time before next daily preparations. Allies gain additional context on successful Recall Knowledge using your report.

description.value: You are so adept at scouting that you no longer need to slow down to do it. When you use the Scout activity you may move at full Speed instead of half Speed.
You dash in, strike your enemy, and then create distance. When you use Scout's Charge, you may also take a Step after you Strike.

Your demoralizing volley has the potential to shatter the enemy's morale. When you use Demoralizing Volley and hit with both Strikes, increase the target's frightened condition by 2 instead of by 1 (to frightened 2 if it does not have the frightened condition). It must also attempt a Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. On a failure, it is fleeing for 1 round.

rules.text: Shattering Volley When both Demoralizing Volley Strikes hit, increase frightened by 2 (not 1), and the target must attempt a Will save or flee for 1 round.

description.value: You are able to craft and explain elaborate ambush plans at a moment's notice. When you use your Scout's Ambush ability and your initiative result is higher than that of all enemy combatants, you may immediately use 2 actions instead of a single action. Additionally you may designate one of your allies within 30 feet. That ally may use a single action immediately as well. You and your ally may choose to take these actions in whichever order you prefer.

rules.text: Legendary Scout's Ambush If your initiative beats all enemies: 2 free actions (not 1), plus designate an ally within 30 ft. who also gets 1 action.

description.value: Your diligent practice has honed your senses beyond what you would normally be capable of. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks while you are using the Scout exploration activity. When you succeed on a Perception check while Scouting, you receive a critical success instead.

You have mastered a magical trick of vanishing completely when you need it the most. You can cast disappearance once per day as an innate occult spell.

You may use this casting of disappearance as a reaction instead of its normal cast time with any the following triggers: you fail your Sneak check as part of Beleaguering Attack; you roll initiative and would gain the status bonus from Spotted!; or you hit both of the Strikes of a Demoralizing Volley. You may only use this reaction once per day, even if you gain additional castings of disappearance from other sources.
